parsed_review.summary: CP00-752 is a descriptor-only Korean Legal Depth evidence/review bridge pack (RP24.P08.M06.S15-RP24.P09.M04.S04, 150 units, risk C). It does NOT assert production_ready: the manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication all consistently report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, verdict PENDING, and pack_level_claude_review status=pending; the named gate flag korean_legal_p08_p09_evidence_review_bridge_descriptor_verified is correctly held false awaiting this review. Independent inspection (Read/Grep/Glob only) confirms the production_ready descriptor claim is supported: (1) distributions are internally consistent and match the baseline exactly — deliverable_counts 16+41+71+10+8+4=150, phase_counts 84+66=150, micro_phase and micro_title row counts sum to 150, and required_section_rows slices yield the expected per-section counts (M06=8, M02=20, M03=22, M04=4); (2) no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and per-row guards in validateDescriptor (writes_product_state/db rows/object storage/external runtime all false; HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate/Korean-clause runtime not opened; permission decision detail and audit event bodies excluded; cross-tenant disallowed); (3) Claude stays read-only and non-final (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, allowed_tools=[Read,Grep,Glob], human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-753); (4) Hermes patterns are deterministic packet/receipt only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write; not embedded in product runtime); (5) CP178+ hardening is encoded (forbidden_review_evidence/invalid_review_blockers cover not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose). The contract regenerates with latest_pack/latest_projection=CP00-752, projections cp722-cp752, validation.valid=true; model.test.js adds CP00-752 binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and contract-projection tests; index.js re-exports via export *. Changed-file scope is clean: active refs are limited to the korean-legal package + contract + validator, and unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/* docs) are correctly segregated with no omitted active untracked implementation files. No secrets, TODO/FIXME, or real-data leaks found. One P3 transparency note about review provenance; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Recommend PASS; final enterprise-trust/runtime approval remains a human authority outside this receipt.

findings.title: Validation-ladder outcomes verified from recorded receipts plus direct source inspection, not independent re-execution
findings.details: As the read-only reviewer I cannot run Bash, so the 10 required validation commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) plus the extra node --check/--test and rp24 commands were confirmed only via the recorded command-evidence.json receipts (all exit_code 0) and the readiness auth_preflight (passed). The PASS additionally rests on direct Read/Grep inspection showing the source, contract projection, and tests are structurally self-consistent (distributions sum to 150, descriptor/coverage validators enforce no-write and read-only boundaries, contract validation.valid=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-753). This is the expected provenance for a read-only review, not a defect in the pack.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping the descriptor-verified gate and closing the pack, the human/CI closeout step should confirm the command-evidence receipts were produced by the actual run on the reviewed tree. No code or artifact change is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged — expected read-only reviewer boundary (no Bash); does not block pack or goal closeout.
